reasoning: The search results confirm that while there are global efforts to develop a Group A streptococcal (Strep A) vaccine (e.g., SAVAC - Strep A Vaccine Global Consortium), candidates are currently only in early phase clinical trials (Phase 1). A phase 1 trial of a peptide-based vaccine is estimated to complete in March 2025, and another nasal vaccine is currently in Phase 1 and advancing to Phase 2. There are absolutely no candidates in Phase 3 trials or undergoing regulatory review for approval right now. Given that vaccine development from Phase 1 to approval typically takes several years, it is virtually impossible that a vaccine for Group A streptococcal infection will have been developed and licensed/verified such that its status on the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page changes to "Yes" by any of the resolution dates (Dec 2025, Jan 2026, or Mar 2026). Therefore, I'm extremely confident that the answer is No for all three resolution dates. I estimate a 2% baseline probability just to account for extreme Wikipedia vandalism or highly unlikely edge cases, but the true biological and regulatory probability is practically 0.
